channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: March Madness tournaments will expand to 76 teams each starting next season
item.description: The NCAA will expand its March Madness tournaments by eight teams each next season. It will add more early-round games in the first week without altering the overall format.
item.content:encoded:
The NCAA will expand its March Madness tournaments by eight teams each next season. It will add more early-round games in the first week without altering the overall format.

(Image credit: Gene J. Puskar)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: North Korea says it will deploy new artillery guns targeting Seoul
item.description: North Korea says it'll deploy new long-range artillery systems capable of striking the capital region of rival South Korea this year and commission its first naval destroyer in coming weeks.
item.content:encoded:
North Korea says it'll deploy new long-range artillery systems capable of striking the capital region of rival South Korea this year and commission its first naval destroyer in coming weeks.

(Image credit: Korean Central News Agency/Korea News Service)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trade court strikes down a second round of Trump tariffs
item.description: The Court of International Trade has struck down a second round of global tariffs ordered by President Trump, after his earlier import taxes were outlawed by the U.S. Supreme Court.
item.content:encoded:
The Court of International Trade has struck down a second round of global tariffs ordered by President Trump, after his earlier import taxes were outlawed by the U.S. Supreme Court.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: U.S. military says it intercepted Iranian attacks on 3 Navy ships in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. military also says that it "targeted Iranian military facilities responsible for attacking U.S. forces." The exchange occurred Thursday as U.S. Navy destroyers transited the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. Central Command said in a social media post.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military also says that it "targeted Iranian military facilities responsible for attacking U.S. forces." The exchange occurred Thursday as U.S. Navy destroyers transited the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. Central Command said in a social media post.

(Image credit: Razieh Poudat/ISNA)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Alcohol warning labels that link drinking to cancer are effective, study says
item.description: A new study finds updated warning labels pointing out the increased risks of specific diseases, like cancer, would do a better job of encouraging people to drink less alcohol than the current warning.
item.content:encoded: A new study finds updated warning labels pointing out the increased risks of specific diseases, like cancer, would do a better job of encouraging people to drink less alcohol than the current warning.
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy

item.title: Is hantavirus the next COVID? Is the U.S. response on point? An outbreak update
item.description: The outbreak began in early April on a cruise ship. Now health authorities around the world are working to contain it. Here's what infectious disease experts have to say.
item.content:encoded:
The outbreak began in early April on a cruise ship. Now health authorities around the world are working to contain it. Here's what infectious disease experts have to say.

(Image credit: Alfred Pasieka/Science Photo Library)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: She helps families in need. As gas and grocery prices rise, she needs help, too
item.description: She has spent decades helping others struggling to make ends meet. Now the rising cost of gas and groceries has left Dalene Basden feeling the strain herself.
item.content:encoded:
She has spent decades helping others struggling to make ends meet. Now the rising cost of gas and groceries has left Dalene Basden feeling the strain herself.

(Image credit: Tovia Smith/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith

item.title: With legal briefs in, Supreme Court weighs telehealth access for the abortion pill
item.description: After an appeals court tried to end telemedicine access to mifepristone, one of the two pills used in a medication abortion, the Supreme Court stepped in. Here's what's happened and what's to come.
item.content:encoded:
After an appeals court tried to end telemedicine access to mifepristone, one of the two pills used in a medication abortion, the Supreme Court stepped in. Here's what's happened and what's to come.

(Image credit: Jonathan Bachman)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: A Trump council recommends overhauling FEMA. Here are 3 key changes
item.description: A group of emergency experts wants the Trump administration to raise the bar for federal help after disasters, and also make it easier for survivors to get money quickly.
item.content:encoded:
A group of emergency experts wants the Trump administration to raise the bar for federal help after disasters, and also make it easier for survivors to get money quickly.

(Image credit: Darren Abate)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Sommer

item.title: Noah Kahan gets a No. 1 album at last, while Michael Jackson reenters the top 10
item.description: This week on the charts, Noah Kahan's coronation as an A-list superstar is complete, while another artist makes serious waves as well: the late Michael Jackson.
item.content:encoded:
This week on the charts, Noah Kahan's coronation as an A-list superstar is complete, while another artist makes serious waves as well: the late Michael Jackson.

(Image credit: Photo by Griffin Lotz/Rolling Stone via Getty Images, Photo by Gary Gershoff/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Thompson
